articles.title: A humanoid robot sprints past the human half-marathon world record in Beijing race
articles.summary: The winner from Honor, a Chinese smartphone maker, completed the race in 50 minutes and 26 seconds, running faster than the human world record in a show of China's technological leaps.

articles.title: Democrat Betty Yee Leaves the California Governor’s Race
articles.summary: Xavier Becerra, who once struggled to gain traction, has found growing support after Eric Swalwell left the race amid sexual harassment allegations.

articles.title: Trump and Iran Face Off in Iran War Negotiations
articles.summary: As the United States and Iran make a second attempt at a deal, their negotiating styles are on a collision course.

articles.title: Tim Cook Will Step Down as Apple C.E.O.
articles.summary: The longtime leader of the iPhone maker will be replaced by John Ternus, the company’s head of hardware engineering.

articles.title: Canada's PM Carney indicates a move away from economic reliance on the U.S.
articles.summary: Canada's close economic ties with the U.S. are now a "weakness," not a strength, Prime Minister Mark Carney says. In a video address to the nation, he outlines plans to expand trade beyond America.

articles.title: The Onion has agreed to a new deal to take over Infowars
articles.summary: The Onion says it has a new deal to take over conspiracy theorist Alex Jones's Infowars media company. If approved, the satirical news website could turn Infowars into a parody of itself.

articles.title: The Touska, the Iranian-Flagged Ship Seized by U.S. Forces, Was Under Sanctions
articles.summary: Marines are searching thousands of containers aboard the Touska, an Iranian cargo ship that the Navy disabled and seized on Sunday.

articles.title: A New Apple C.E.O.
articles.summary: Also, JD Vance is set to travel to Pakistan for peace talks. Here’s the latest at the end of Monday.

articles.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO. In letter, describes 15 years of emails
articles.summary: The 65-year-old Cook will turn over CEO duties to Apple's head of hardware products, John Ternus, in September. Cook will remain with the company as executive chairman.

articles.title: Trump's labor secretary resigns amid investigation into misconduct
articles.summary: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer, who was under internal investigation, is leaving her position. She becomes the third cabinet departure of President Trump's second term.

articles.title: Who Is John Ternus, Apple’s Low-Profile Leader?
articles.summary: John Ternus, a low-profile but influential executive at Apple, could be next in line to replace the company’s longtime chief executive, Tim Cook, if he steps aside.

articles.title: U.S.-Iran talks are uncertain as ceasefire is set to expire
articles.summary: As the clock ticks down on the ceasefire, Iran says it may not attend fresh talks with the U.S. That's as a temporary ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon appears to be holding.

articles.title: Kash Patel Sues The Atlantic for $250 Million Over Article Claiming Excessive Drinking
articles.summary: Mr. Patel accused the publication of defamation, asking for $250 million in damages. A spokeswoman for The Atlantic called the suit “meritless.”

articles.title: The 11 most challenged books of 2025, according to the American Library Association
articles.summary: The ALA says 4,235 titles were challenged at U.S. libraries — the second-highest year on record. Forty percent of the challenged works involved LGBTQ+ subjects or the experiences of people of color.

articles.title: Israeli Soldier in Lebanon Sledgehammered a Statue of Jesus
articles.summary: The military is investigating the soldier. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu expressed regret for any hurt caused to “believers in Lebanon and around the world.”

articles.title: Peace talks are in doubt as the U.S. seizes an Iranian ship
articles.summary: President Trump said a U.S. delegation will head to Pakistan to resume talks to end the war with Iran, but Tehran expressed reluctance after the U.S. seized one of its cargo ships in the Strait of Hor

articles.title: A mine despoiled the beauty of the rainforest. This Goldman Prize winner took action
articles.summary: "We women are the land guardians and keepers," says Theonila Roka Matbob of Papua New Guinea, recognized for her efforts to repair the environmental and social harms caused by a copper and gold mine.

articles.title: U.S. seizes Iranian cargo ship. And, tariff refund portal launches
articles.summary: U.S. forces seized an Iranian-flagged cargo vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. And, an online government portal for processing tariff refunds launches today.

articles.title: Got wearable data? Your doctor can help you connect the dots
articles.summary: The American Academy of Neurology issued guidance on using wearable data devices, like smartwatches or an Oura Ring, to track key health metrics that can help flag serious conditions.

articles.title: After massive cuts, U.S. says HIV work abroad is going well, but experts disagree
articles.summary: The Trump administration has released new data on the country's HIV/AIDS work abroad. It is claiming the numbers are good news, but many HIV experts say they paint a worrisome picture.

articles.title: Ukraine's fast-growing defense tech sector takes center stage in Iran war
articles.summary: While Ukraine seeks to shift its image from not only a recipient of military aid but also a provider, its battle-tested, anti-drone tech is taking center stage in the Iran war.

articles.title: U.K police investigating if attacks in London are the work of Iranian proxies
articles.summary: U.K. police said Sunday they are investigating whether a string of arson attacks on Jewish sites in London are the work of Iranian proxies, the latest of which caused minor damage on Saturday night.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
